name: Crispie Coconut Refrigerator Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 20 Minutes
Chill Time: 2 hours to Overnight Bake
Time: 12 Minutes
Hint: Cookie rolls can be frozen for up to three months.
1. In a large bowl, cream the Butter Flavor Crisco and sugar with an electric mixer on medium speed until well blended. Beat in the egg.
2. Sift together the All Purpose Flour, salt, and baking soda into another bowl. Add this mixture to the creamed butter and sugar, alternating with 2 cups of the flaked coconut, mixing until just combined.
3. Divide the dough in half. Roll each half into a roll about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
4. Place pieces of wax paper (18 inches long) on a tray. Sprinkle 1/2 cup coconut onto each piece of wax paper. Wrap each dough roll around the wax paper, rolling it up tightly. Refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
5. Preheat the oven to 325°F. Grease a baking sheet with Butter Flavor Crisco and set aside.
6. Cut the chilled rolls into 1/4 inch slices using a sharp knife. Place each slice about 2 inches apart on the prepared baking sheet.
7. Whisk together the egg yolk and milk. Brush this mixture onto each cookie slice, then place a pecan half on top of each slice.
8. Bake in the preheated oven for 12 minutes. Remove from the oven and let cool on a cooling rack.
Makes 6 dozen cookies, each about 1 1/2 inches long.
